Biophi server tutorial

Installing the server

Normally you will not need this section, but I will leave it there, just in case it ever will be needed

  1. Open the terminal window Ctrl + Alt + T
  2. First it was necessary to install wget. To do this in the terminal type the following command.

    sudo apt-get install wget

  3. After wget is installed you will need to download the database file. Type the command below:

    wget https://zenodo.org/record/5164685/files/OASis_9mers_v1.db.gz

  4. Unzip the database

    gunzip OASis_9mers_v1.db.gz

  5. Create the environment.

    conda create -n biophi python=3.9

    In this command biophi is the name of the environment. It is not compulsory to call it biophi, it can have any name as long as it is one word and starts with letter, not a number. If you already have another environment with the name biophi, you won't be able to create the new environment with the same name.

  6. Activate the environment:

    conda activate biophi

    If you called your environment differently, not biophi adjust your command accordingly.

    It needs to be conda activate environment_name

  7. Install biophi conda install biophi -c bioconda -c conda-forge --override-channels
  8. Skip to the second point of the "Using Biophi, if Biophi is already installed" section

Using Biophi, if Biophi is already installed

  1. Activate the environment:

    conda activate biophi

  2. Set up the path to OASIS database. You should know where database is located, noramlly it automatically downloads to the home directory.

    You should run the command similar to the one below, except your path will be different to the one shown below:

    export OASIS_DB_PATH=/path/to/downloaded/OASis_9mers_v1.db

    Currently the database is in the home directory, so you can run just:

    export OASIS_DB_PATH=OASis_9mers_v1.db

  3. Finally, run the command:

    biophi web

    You will see something similar to the picture below

  4. Copy the link highlighted in the picture in blue to the browser
  5. Once the server has loaded you will have the following options
    • Humannes report
    • Humanise

Evaluating the humannes of the nanobody using biophi server

  1. To Evaluate the humannes of the nanobody click on Humannes report
  2. Enter the nanobody sequence to the "heavy chain" window

  3. Change the numbering and CDR definition to IMGT. Choose OASIS prevalence treshold of >= 10% (relaxed)

  4. press submit

Understanding the humannes evaluation results

Let's look athe basic results summary below:


To get to more extensive overview, you need to press button


The concept of the OASIS Identity score

To get the OASIS identity score of the nanobody or antibody, antobody is cut in the 9 amino acid long fragments using sliding window approach.

The screenshot below, explains what this means, if you look at the "peptide" column, it contains the 9 amino acid peptide starting with the certain amino acid position.

The full sequence of the nanobody is below:

EVQLQASGGGLVQPGGSLRLSCVASGIDFSTNVPTWYRQAPGKQRDLVAVITTNGLTRYADS VKGRFTISKDNAKNAVYLQMNSLKPEDTAVYYCNAFVGLTDYWGQGTQVTVSS

Each position except the last 8 amino acids in the nanobody gets peptide assocaited with it

To be classified as human, the peptide should be present in the fraction of subjects (human donors) specified by the prevalence treshold.

In the example above we specified the prevalence treshold to be 10%, which means that only peptides that are present in the repertuare at least 10% of human donors will be classified as human.

The OASIS identity is the ratio of the number of the human peptides at given prevalence threshold, to number of all peptides obtained from the sequence. This is the main score we use for estimation of the humannes.

For instance, in the example above the OASIS identity is 51%, because 55 of 107 peptides are considered human (are found in at least 10% of human subjects). 55/107 ≈ 0.51.

The other metrics that Biophi servers computes are:

  • OASis Percentile - what percent of the theurapeutic antibodies would score the same oasis identity given the same prevalence threshold. In the example above if 10% threshold would be applied to the reference therapeutic antibody database 8% would result in 51% OASIS identity score.
  • chain frequency - residue frequency in the heavy chain of the antibody.
  • residue frequency in the closest germline family at given position.
  • percentage of the human subjects (donors) who have that peptide in their repertoire
  • the total number of sequences in the database that have that certain peptide

Using biophi for humanisation

The interface is very similar to the previous section

Input your sequence in the heavy chain window

Switch the numbering to IMGT, make sure that humanisation algorithm is Sapiens

Choose whether or not you want to humanise the CDRs

Choose humannes evaluation, the threshold we typically use is >= 10%

Press submit


If you click on you will see how the metric parameters descrived in the evaulate humannes section changed and the proposed mutations

The example is shown below:


You can export your data using the export button on top of the page

Biophi github

BioPhi: A platform for antibody design, humanization, and humanness evaluation based on natural antibody repertoires and deep learning.
